SEARCH
Package de.jvstvshd.necrify.api.punishment

Interface PunishmentManager

All Known Implementing Classes:
DefaultPunishmentManager
@Deprecated(since="1.2.0", forRemoval=true) @ScheduledForRemoval(inVersion="1.3.0") public interface PunishmentManager
Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
This interface's functionality will be superseded by the new NecrifyUser.
An interface for managing punishments.
See Also:

  • Method Details

    • createBan

      Ban createBan(UUID player, net.kyori.adventure.text.Component reason, PunishmentDuration duration)
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Prepares a ban for a player with custom reason and duration.
      Parameters:
      player - the uuid of the player which should be banned (by either {link Player#getUniqueId() or PlayerResolver.getPlayerUuid(String).
      reason - the reason given as a Component.
      duration - the duration, which can be created via PunishmentDuration.parse(String) when it's source is from minecraft commands.
      Returns:
      the prepared ban with the given reason and duration. This duration remains the same at any duration since it is only added when the player is banned. Only Punishment.punish() is needed to execute the punishment.

    • createPermanentBan

      default Ban createPermanentBan(UUID player, net.kyori.adventure.text.Component reason)
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Prepares a ban for a player with custom reason and duration. Its only difference to createBan(UUID, Component, PunishmentDuration) is, that the PunishmentDuration is PunishmentDuration.permanent().
      Parameters:
      player - the uuid of the player which should be banned (by either Player#getUniqueId() ()} or PlayerResolver.getPlayerUuid(String).
      reason - the reason given as a Component.
      Returns:
      the prepared ban with the given reason and duration. The duration is permanent, equals LocalDateTime.MAX Only Punishment.punish() is needed to execute the punishment.

    • createMute

      Mute createMute(UUID player, net.kyori.adventure.text.Component reason, PunishmentDuration duration)
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Prepares a mute for a player with custom reason and duration.
      Parameters:
      player - the uuid of the player which should be banned (by either Player#getUniqueId() or PlayerResolver.getPlayerUuid(String).
      reason - the reason given as a Component.
      duration - the duration, which can be created via PunishmentDuration.parse(String) when it's source is from minecraft commands.
      Returns:
      the prepared ban with the given reason and duration. This duration remains the same at any duration since it is only added when the player is banned. Only Punishment.punish() is needed to execute the punishment.

    • createPermanentMute

      default Mute createPermanentMute(UUID player, net.kyori.adventure.text.Component reason)
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Prepares a ban for a player with custom reason and duration. Its only difference to createMute(UUID, Component, PunishmentDuration) is, that the PunishmentDuration is PunishmentDuration.permanent().
      Parameters:
      player - the uuid of the player which should be banned (by either Player#getUniqueId() or PlayerResolver.getPlayerUuid(String)
      reason - the reason given as a Component.
      Returns:
      the prepared ban with the given reason and duration. The duration is permanent, equals LocalDateTime.MAX Only Punishment.punish() is needed to execute the punishment.

    • getPunishments

      <T extends Punishment> CompletableFuture<List<T>> getPunishments(UUID player, Executor service, PunishmentType... type)
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      This method queries all punishments with the given UUID of a player and returns them in a list.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of punishment(s), matching them in type
      Parameters:
      player - the player whose punishments should be queried
      Returns:
      the list of punishments which are stored at the moment. This list may contains punishments which are over.

    • getPunishment

      <T extends Punishment> CompletableFuture<Optional<T>> getPunishment(UUID punishmentId, Executor service)
      Deprecated.
      This method is deprecated and will be removed in 1.3.0. Use Necrify.getPunishment(UUID) instead.
      Queries the punishment stored with the given punishmentId
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of punishment
      Parameters:
      punishmentId - the punishment id from the punishment that should be queried
      service - an Executor which will be used to perform async operations
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the queried punishment or Optional.empty() if it was not found

    • isBanned

      default CompletableFuture<Boolean> isBanned(UUID playerUuid, Executor executor)
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Checks whether the player specified via playerUuid. By default this method must not be overwritten and throws an UnsupportedOperationException
      Parameters:
      playerUuid - the uuid of the player
      executor - an Executor used for async operations
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture, being completed with true if this player is banned and false if not
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if the method was not overwritten
